Problem Statement: In the following exercises, state whether each statement is true, or give an example to show that it is false.
Question: If $$\sum_{n = 1}^{\infty} a_{n} x^{n}$$ converges, then $$a_{n} x^{n} \vec{} 0$$ as $$n \vec{} \infty .$$
Answer: True. If a series converges then its terms tend to zero.

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, state whether each statement is true, or give an example to show that it is false.
Question: $$\sum_{n = 1}^{\infty} a_{n} x^{n}$$ converges at $$x = 0$$ for any real numbers $$a_{n} .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, state whether each statement is true, or give an example to show that it is false.
Question: Given any sequence $$a_{n} ,$$ there is always some $$R > 0 ,$$ possibly very small, such that $$\sum_{n = 1}^{\infty} a_{n} x^{n}$$ converges on $$( \text{unicode[:-]} R , R ) .$$
Answer: False. It would imply that $$a_{n} x^{n} \vec{} 0$$ for $$| x | < R .$$ If $$a_{n} = n^{n} ,$$ then $$a_{n} x^{n} = ( n x )^{n}$$ does not tend to zero for any $$x \ne 0 .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, state whether each statement is true, or give an example to show that it is false.
Question: If $$\sum_{n = 1}^{\infty} a_{n} x^{n}$$ has radius of convergence $$R > 0$$ and if $$| b_{n} | \le | a_{n} |$$ for all n , then the radius of convergence of $$\sum_{n = 1}^{\infty} b_{n} x^{n}$$ is greater than or equal to R .

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, state whether each statement is true, or give an example to show that it is false.
Question: Suppose that $$\sum_{n = 0}^{\infty} a_{n} ( x - 3 )^{n}$$ converges at $$x = 6 .$$ At which of the following points might the series diverge? Use the fact that if $$\sum a_{n} ( x - c )^{n}$$ converges at x , then it converges at any point closer to c than x . $$x = 1$$ $$x = 2$$ $$x = 3$$ $$x = 0$$ $$x = 5.99$$ $$x = 0.000001$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, state whether each statement is true, or give an example to show that it is false.
Question: Suppose that $$\sum_{n = 0}^{\infty} a_{n} ( x + 1 )^{n}$$ converges at $$x = -2 .$$ At which of the following points must the series also converge? Use the fact that if $$\sum a_{n} ( x - c )^{n}$$ converges at x , then it converges at any point closer to c than x . $$x = 2$$ $$x = -1$$ $$x = -3$$ $$x = 0$$ $$x = 0.99$$ $$x = 0.000001$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, suppose that $$| \frac{a_{n + 1}}{a_{n}} | \vec{} 1$$ as $$n \vec{} \infty .$$ Find the radius of convergence for each series.
Question: $$\sum_{n = 0}^{\infty} a_{n} 2^{n} x^{n}$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, suppose that $$| \frac{a_{n + 1}}{a_{n}} | \vec{} 1$$ as $$n \vec{} \infty .$$ Find the radius of convergence for each series.
Question: $$\sum_{n = 0}^{\infty} \frac{a_{n} x^{n}}{2^{n}}$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, suppose that $$| \frac{a_{n + 1}}{a_{n}} | \vec{} 1$$ as $$n \vec{} \infty .$$ Find the radius of convergence for each series.
Question: $$\sum_{n = 0}^{\infty} \frac{a_{n} \pi^{n} x^{n}}{e^{n}}$$
Answer: $$| \frac{a_{n + 1} ( \frac{\pi}{e} )^{n + 1} x^{n + 1}}{a_{n} ( \frac{\pi}{e} )^{n} x^{n}} | = \frac{\pi | x |}{e} | \frac{a_{n + 1}}{a_{n}} | \vec{} \frac{\pi | x |}{e}$$ so $$R = \frac{e}{\pi}$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, suppose that $$| \frac{a_{n + 1}}{a_{n}} | \vec{} 1$$ as $$n \vec{} \infty .$$ Find the radius of convergence for each series.
Question: $$\sum_{n = 0}^{\infty} \frac{a_{n} ( -1 )^{n} x^{n}}{10^{n}}$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, suppose that $$| \frac{a_{n + 1}}{a_{n}} | \vec{} 1$$ as $$n \vec{} \infty .$$ Find the radius of convergence for each series.
Question: $$\sum_{n = 0}^{\infty} a_{n} ( -1 )^{n} x^{2 n}$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, suppose that $$| \frac{a_{n + 1}}{a_{n}} | \vec{} 1$$ as $$n \vec{} \infty .$$ Find the radius of convergence for each series.
Question: $$\sum_{n = 0}^{\infty} a_{n} ( -4 )^{n} x^{2 n}$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, find the radius of convergence R and interval of convergence for $$\sum a_{n} x^{n}$$ with the given coefficients $$a_{n} .$$
Question: $$\sum_{n = 1}^{\infty} \frac{( 2 x )^{n}}{n}$$
Answer: $$a_{n} = \frac{2^{n}}{n}$$ so $$\frac{a_{n + 1} x}{a_{n}} \vec{} 2 x .$$ so $$R = \frac{1}{2} .$$ When $$x = \frac{1}{2}$$ the series is harmonic and diverges. When $$x = - \frac{1}{2}$$ the series is alternating harmonic and converges. The interval of convergence is $$I = [ - \frac{1}{2} , \frac{1}{2} ) .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, find the radius of convergence R and interval of convergence for $$\sum a_{n} x^{n}$$ with the given coefficients $$a_{n} .$$
Question: $$\sum_{n = 1}^{\infty} ( -1 )^{n} \frac{x^{n}}{\sqrt{n}}$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, find the radius of convergence R and interval of convergence for $$\sum a_{n} x^{n}$$ with the given coefficients $$a_{n} .$$
Question: $$\sum_{n = 1}^{\infty} \frac{n x^{n}}{2^{n}}$$
Answer: $$a_{n} = \frac{n}{2^{n}}$$ so $$\frac{a_{n + 1} x}{a_{n}} \vec{} \frac{x}{2}$$ so $$R = 2 .$$ When $$x = \text{unicode[:+-]} 2$$ the series diverges by the divergence test. The interval of convergence is $$I = ( -2 , 2 ) .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, find the radius of convergence R and interval of convergence for $$\sum a_{n} x^{n}$$ with the given coefficients $$a_{n} .$$
Question: $$\sum_{n = 1}^{\infty} \frac{n x^{n}}{e^{n}}$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, find the radius of convergence R and interval of convergence for $$\sum a_{n} x^{n}$$ with the given coefficients $$a_{n} .$$
Question: $$\sum_{n = 1}^{\infty} \frac{n^{2} x^{n}}{2^{n}}$$
Answer: $$a_{n} = \frac{n^{2}}{2^{n}}$$ so $$R = 2 .$$ When $$x = \text{unicode[:+-]} 2$$ the series diverges by the divergence test. The interval of convergence is $$I = ( -2 , 2 ) .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, find the radius of convergence R and interval of convergence for $$\sum a_{n} x^{n}$$ with the given coefficients $$a_{n} .$$
Question: $$\sum_{k = 1}^{\infty} \frac{k^{e} x^{k}}{e^{k}}$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, find the radius of convergence R and interval of convergence for $$\sum a_{n} x^{n}$$ with the given coefficients $$a_{n} .$$
Question: $$\sum_{k = 1}^{\infty} \frac{\pi^{k} x^{k}}{k^{\pi}}$$
Answer: $$a_{k} = \frac{\pi^{k}}{k^{\pi}}$$ so $$R = \frac{1}{\pi} .$$ When $$x = \text{unicode[:+-]} \frac{1}{\pi}$$ the series is an absolutely convergent p -series. The interval of convergence is $$I = [ - \frac{1}{\pi} , \frac{1}{\pi} ] .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, find the radius of convergence R and interval of convergence for $$\sum a_{n} x^{n}$$ with the given coefficients $$a_{n} .$$
Question: $$\sum_{n = 1}^{\infty} \frac{x^{n}}{n \text{!}}$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, find the radius of convergence R and interval of convergence for $$\sum a_{n} x^{n}$$ with the given coefficients $$a_{n} .$$
Question: $$\sum_{n = 1}^{\infty} \frac{10^{n} x^{n}}{n \text{!}}$$
Answer: $$a_{n} = \frac{10^{n}}{n \text{!}} , \frac{a_{n + 1} x}{a_{n}} = \frac{10 x}{n + 1} \vec{} 0 < 1$$ so the series converges for all x by the ratio test and $$I = ( \text{unicode[:-]} \infty , \infty ) .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, find the radius of convergence R and interval of convergence for $$\sum a_{n} x^{n}$$ with the given coefficients $$a_{n} .$$
Question: $$\sum_{n = 1}^{\infty} ( -1 )^{n} \frac{x^{n}}{\text{ln} ( 2 n )}$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, find the radius of convergence of each series.
Question: $$\sum_{k = 1}^{\infty} \frac{( k \text{!} )^{2} x^{k}}{( 2 k ) \text{!}}$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, find the radius of convergence of each series.
Question: $$\sum_{n = 1}^{\infty} \frac{( 2 n ) \text{!} x^{n}}{n^{2 n}}$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, find the radius of convergence of each series.
Question: $$\sum_{k = 1}^{\infty} \frac{k \text{!}}{1 \cdotp 3 \cdotp 5 \text{cdots} ( 2 k - 1 )} x^{k}$$
Answer: $$a_{k} = \frac{k \text{!}}{1 \cdotp 3 \cdotp 5 \text{cdots} ( 2 k - 1 )}$$ so $$\frac{a_{k + 1}}{a_{k}} = \frac{k + 1}{2 k + 1} \vec{} \frac{1}{2}$$ so $$R = 2$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, find the radius of convergence of each series.
Question: $$\sum_{k = 1}^{\infty} \frac{2 \cdotp 4 \cdotp 6 \text{cdots} 2 k}{( 2 k ) \text{!}} x^{k}$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, find the radius of convergence of each series.
Question: $$\sum_{n = 1}^{\infty} \frac{x^{n}}{\left (\begin{matrix}2 n \\ n\end{matrix}\right )}$$ where $$\left (\begin{matrix}n \\ k\end{matrix}\right ) = \frac{n \text{!}}{k \text{!} ( n - k ) \text{!}}$$
Answer: $$a_{n} = \frac{1}{\left (\begin{matrix}2 n \\ n\end{matrix}\right )}$$ so $$\frac{a_{n + 1}}{a_{n}} = \frac{( ( n + 1 ) \text{!} )^{2}}{( 2 n + 2 ) \text{!}} \frac{2 n \text{!}}{( n \text{!} )^{2}} = \frac{( n + 1 )^{2}}{( 2 n + 2 ) ( 2 n + 1 )} \vec{} \frac{1}{4}$$ so $$R = 4$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, find the radius of convergence of each series.
Question: $$\sum_{n = 1}^{\infty} \text{sin}^{2} n x^{n}$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, use the ratio test to determine the radius of convergence of each series.
Question: $$\sum_{n = 1}^{\infty} \frac{( n \text{!} )^{3}}{( 3 n ) \text{!}} x^{n}$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, use the ratio test to determine the radius of convergence of each series.
Question: $$\sum_{n = 1}^{\infty} \frac{2^{3 n} ( n \text{!} )^{3}}{( 3 n ) \text{!}} x^{n}$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, use the ratio test to determine the radius of convergence of each series.
Question: $$\sum_{n = 1}^{\infty} \frac{n \text{!}}{n^{n}} x^{n}$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, use the ratio test to determine the radius of convergence of each series.
Question: $$\sum_{n = 1}^{\infty} \frac{( 2 n ) \text{!}}{n^{2 n}} x^{n}$$

Question: $$f ( x ) = \frac{x^{2}}{1 + x^{2}} ; a = 0$$

Question: $$f ( x ) = \frac{x^{2}}{1 - 4 x^{2}} ; a = 0$$

Question: $$f ( x ) = \frac{x^{2}}{5 - 4 x + x^{2}} ; a = 2$$

Problem Statement: Use the next exercise to find the radius of convergence of the given series in the subsequent exercises.
Question: Explain why, if $$| a_{n} |^{1 \text{/} n} \vec{} r > 0 ,$$ then $$| a_{n} x^{n} |^{1 \text{/} n} \vec{} | x | r < 1$$ whenever $$| x | < \frac{1}{r}$$ and, therefore, the radius of convergence of $$\sum_{n = 1}^{\infty} a_{n} x^{n}$$ is $$R = \frac{1}{r} .$$
Answer: $$| a_{n} x^{n} |^{1 \text{/} n} = | a_{n} |^{1 \text{/} n} | x | \vec{} | x | r$$ as $$n \vec{} \infty$$ and $$| x | r < 1$$ when $$| x | < \frac{1}{r} .$$ Therefore, $$\sum_{n = 1}^{\infty} a_{n} x^{n}$$ converges when $$| x | < \frac{1}{r}$$ by the n th root test.

Problem Statement: Use the next exercise to find the radius of convergence of the given series in the subsequent exercises.
Question: Suppose that $$p ( x ) = \sum_{n = 0}^{\infty} a_{n} x^{n}$$ such that $$a_{n} = 0$$ if n is even. Explain why $$p ( x ) = - p ( \text{unicode[:-]} x ) .$$

Problem Statement: Use the next exercise to find the radius of convergence of the given series in the subsequent exercises.
Question: Suppose that $$p ( x ) = \sum_{n = 0}^{\infty} a_{n} x^{n}$$ such that $$a_{n} = 0$$ if n is odd. Explain why $$p ( x ) = p ( \text{unicode[:-]} x ) .$$
Answer: We can rewrite $$p ( x ) = \sum_{n = 0}^{\infty} a_{2 n + 1} x^{2 n + 1}$$ and $$p ( x ) = p ( \text{unicode[:-]} x )$$ since only even powers of $$x$$ remain, $$p ( x )$$ is an even function, for which, by definition $$p ( x ) = p ( -x )$$ .

Problem Statement: Use the next exercise to find the radius of convergence of the given series in the subsequent exercises.
Question: Suppose that $$p ( x ) = \sum_{n = 0}^{\infty} a_{n} x^{n}$$ converges on $$( -1 , 1 ] .$$ Find the interval of convergence of $$p ( A x ) .$$

Problem Statement: Use the next exercise to find the radius of convergence of the given series in the subsequent exercises.
Question: Suppose that $$p ( x ) = \sum_{n = 0}^{\infty} a_{n} x^{n}$$ converges on $$( -1 , 1 ] .$$ Find the interval of convergence of $$p ( 2 x - 1 ) .$$
Answer: If $$x \in [ 0 , 1 ] ,$$ then $$y = 2 x - 1 \in [ -1 , 1 ]$$ so $$p ( 2 x - 1 ) = p ( y ) = \sum_{n = 0}^{\infty} a_{n} y^{n}$$ converges.

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, suppose that $$p ( x ) = \sum_{n = 0}^{\infty} a_{n} x^{n}$$ satisfies $$\underset{n \rightarrow \infty}{\text{lim}} \frac{a_{n + 1}}{a_{n}} = 1$$ where $$a_{n} \geq 0$$ for each n . State whether each series converges on the full interval $$( -1 , 1 ) ,$$ or if there is not enough information to draw a conclusion. Use the comparison test when appropriate.
Question: $$\sum_{n = 0}^{\infty} a_{n} x^{2 n}$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, suppose that $$p ( x ) = \sum_{n = 0}^{\infty} a_{n} x^{n}$$ satisfies $$\underset{n \rightarrow \infty}{\text{lim}} \frac{a_{n + 1}}{a_{n}} = 1$$ where $$a_{n} \geq 0$$ for each n . State whether each series converges on the full interval $$( -1 , 1 ) ,$$ or if there is not enough information to draw a conclusion. Use the comparison test when appropriate.
Question: $$\sum_{n = 0}^{\infty} a_{2 n} x^{2 n}$$
Answer: Converges on $$( -1 , 1 )$$ by the ratio test

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, suppose that $$p ( x ) = \sum_{n = 0}^{\infty} a_{n} x^{n}$$ satisfies $$\underset{n \rightarrow \infty}{\text{lim}} \frac{a_{n + 1}}{a_{n}} = 1$$ where $$a_{n} \geq 0$$ for each n . State whether each series converges on the full interval $$( -1 , 1 ) ,$$ or if there is not enough information to draw a conclusion. Use the comparison test when appropriate.
Question: $$\sum_{n = 0}^{\infty} a_{2 n} x^{n} ( H i n t \text{:} x = \text{unicode[:+-]} \sqrt{x^{2}} )$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, suppose that $$p ( x ) = \sum_{n = 0}^{\infty} a_{n} x^{n}$$ satisfies $$\underset{n \rightarrow \infty}{\text{lim}} \frac{a_{n + 1}}{a_{n}} = 1$$ where $$a_{n} \geq 0$$ for each n . State whether each series converges on the full interval $$( -1 , 1 ) ,$$ or if there is not enough information to draw a conclusion. Use the comparison test when appropriate.
Question: $$\sum_{n = 0}^{\infty} a_{n^{2}} x^{n^{2}}$$ ( Hint: Let $$b_{k} = a_{k}$$ if $$k = n^{2}$$ for some n , otherwise $$b_{k} = 0 . )$$
Answer: Consider the series $$\sum b_{k} x^{k}$$ where $$b_{k} = a_{k}$$ if $$k = n^{2}$$ and $$b_{k} = 0$$ otherwise. Then $$b_{k} \le a_{k}$$ and so the series converges on $$( -1 , 1 )$$ by the comparison test.

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, suppose that $$p ( x ) = \sum_{n = 0}^{\infty} a_{n} x^{n}$$ satisfies $$\underset{n \rightarrow \infty}{\text{lim}} \frac{a_{n + 1}}{a_{n}} = 1$$ where $$a_{n} \geq 0$$ for each n . State whether each series converges on the full interval $$( -1 , 1 ) ,$$ or if there is not enough information to draw a conclusion. Use the comparison test when appropriate.
Question: Suppose that $$p ( x )$$ is a polynomial of degree N . Find the radius and interval of convergence of $$\sum_{n = 1}^{\infty} p ( n ) x^{n} .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, suppose that $$p ( x ) = \sum_{n = 0}^{\infty} a_{n} x^{n}$$ satisfies $$\underset{n \rightarrow \infty}{\text{lim}} \frac{a_{n + 1}}{a_{n}} = 1$$ where $$a_{n} \geq 0$$ for each n . State whether each series converges on the full interval $$( -1 , 1 ) ,$$ or if there is not enough information to draw a conclusion. Use the comparison test when appropriate.
Question: [T] Plot the graphs of $$\frac{1}{1 - x}$$ and of the partial sums $$S_{N} = \sum_{n = 0}^{N} x^{n}$$ for $$n = 10 , 20 , 30$$ on the interval $$[ -0.99 , 0.99 ] .$$ Comment on the approximation of $$\frac{1}{1 - x}$$ by $$S_{N}$$ near $$x = -1$$ and near $$x = 1$$ as N increases.
Answer: The approximation is more accurate near $$x = -1 .$$ The partial sums follow $$\frac{1}{1 - x}$$ more closely as N increases but are never accurate near $$x = 1$$ since the series diverges there.

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, suppose that $$p ( x ) = \sum_{n = 0}^{\infty} a_{n} x^{n}$$ satisfies $$\underset{n \rightarrow \infty}{\text{lim}} \frac{a_{n + 1}}{a_{n}} = 1$$ where $$a_{n} \geq 0$$ for each n . State whether each series converges on the full interval $$( -1 , 1 ) ,$$ or if there is not enough information to draw a conclusion. Use the comparison test when appropriate.
Question: [T] Plot the graphs of $$\text{unicode[:-]} \text{ln} ( 1 - x )$$ and of the partial sums $$S_{N} = \sum_{n = 1}^{N} \frac{x^{n}}{n}$$ for $$n = 10 , 50 , 100$$ on the interval $$[ -0.99 , 0.99 ] .$$ Comment on the behavior of the sums near $$x = -1$$ and near $$x = 1$$ as N increases.

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, suppose that $$p ( x ) = \sum_{n = 0}^{\infty} a_{n} x^{n}$$ satisfies $$\underset{n \rightarrow \infty}{\text{lim}} \frac{a_{n + 1}}{a_{n}} = 1$$ where $$a_{n} \geq 0$$ for each n . State whether each series converges on the full interval $$( -1 , 1 ) ,$$ or if there is not enough information to draw a conclusion. Use the comparison test when appropriate.
Question: [T] Plot the graphs of the partial sums $$S_{n} = \sum_{n = 1}^{N} \frac{x^{n}}{n^{2}}$$ for $$n = 10 , 50 , 100$$ on the interval $$[ -0.99 , 0.99 ] .$$ Comment on the behavior of the sums near $$x = -1$$ and near $$x = 1$$ as N increases.
Answer: The approximation appears to stabilize quickly near both $$x = \text{unicode[:+-]} 1 .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, suppose that $$p ( x ) = \sum_{n = 0}^{\infty} a_{n} x^{n}$$ satisfies $$\underset{n \rightarrow \infty}{\text{lim}} \frac{a_{n + 1}}{a_{n}} = 1$$ where $$a_{n} \geq 0$$ for each n . State whether each series converges on the full interval $$( -1 , 1 ) ,$$ or if there is not enough information to draw a conclusion. Use the comparison test when appropriate.
Question: [T] Plot the graphs of the partial sums $$S_{N} = \sum_{n = 1}^{N} \text{sin} n x^{n}$$ for $$n = 10 , 50 , 100$$ on the interval $$[ -0.99 , 0.99 ] .$$ Comment on the behavior of the sums near $$x = -1$$ and near $$x = 1$$ as N increases.

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, suppose that $$p ( x ) = \sum_{n = 0}^{\infty} a_{n} x^{n}$$ satisfies $$\underset{n \rightarrow \infty}{\text{lim}} \frac{a_{n + 1}}{a_{n}} = 1$$ where $$a_{n} \geq 0$$ for each n . State whether each series converges on the full interval $$( -1 , 1 ) ,$$ or if there is not enough information to draw a conclusion. Use the comparison test when appropriate.
Question: [T] Plot the graphs of the partial sums $$S_{N} = \sum_{n = 0}^{N} ( -1 )^{n} \frac{x^{2 n + 1}}{( 2 n + 1 ) \text{!}}$$ for $$n = 3 , 5 , 10$$ on the interval $$[ -2 \pi , 2 \pi ] .$$ Comment on how these plots approximate $$\text{sin} x$$ as N increases.
Answer: The polynomial curves have roots close to those of $$\text{sin} x$$ up to their degree and then the polynomials diverge from $$\text{sin} x .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, suppose that $$p ( x ) = \sum_{n = 0}^{\infty} a_{n} x^{n}$$ satisfies $$\underset{n \rightarrow \infty}{\text{lim}} \frac{a_{n + 1}}{a_{n}} = 1$$ where $$a_{n} \geq 0$$ for each n . State whether each series converges on the full interval $$( -1 , 1 ) ,$$ or if there is not enough information to draw a conclusion. Use the comparison test when appropriate.
Question: [T] Plot the graphs of the partial sums $$S_{N} = \sum_{n = 0}^{N} ( -1 )^{n} \frac{x^{2 n}}{( 2 n ) \text{!}}$$ for $$n = 3 , 5 , 10$$ on the interval $$[ -2 \pi , 2 \pi ] .$$ Comment on how these plots approximate $$\text{cos} x$$ as N increases.
